{"method":"item/started","params":{"item":{"type":"exitedReviewMode","id":"019dd6d5-91fa-7820-acb5-8ebd37da76e4","review":"The thread draft preservation path fixes the covered scenario, but the new async paste handling drops completed image attachments for launchpad composers if the user navigates away before normalization finishes.

Review comment:

- [P2] Preserve async pasted images for launchpad scopes — /Users/huntharo/github/PwrAgnt/.worktrees/launchpad-pwragnt-main-moja6ucz/apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/features/composer/Composer.tsx:971-979
  When an image paste starts from a new-thread launchpad and the user switches away before `normalizeImageFile` resolves, this branch runs with a `launchpad:*` `pasteScope.key`; `saveThreadComposerDraft` ignores non-thread scopes, so the completed attachment is dropped instead of being persisted back to the launchpad draft. This makes large pasted images silently disappear for launchpad composers under the same switch-away race this change is trying to fix."},"threadId":"019dd682-56d6-7601-8634-fc3a49e67554","turnId":"019dd6d5-91fa-7820-acb5-8ebd37da76e4"}}

This has been regularly failing for me.  So let's catch it, then let's fix it.","ephemeral":false,"modelProvider":"openai","createdAt":1777420359,"updatedAt":1777425951,"status":{"type":"idle"},"path":"/Users/huntharo/.codex/sessions/2026/04/28/rollout-2026-04-28T19-52-39-019dd682-56d6-7601-8634-fc3a49e67554.jsonl","cwd":"/Users/huntharo/github/PwrAgnt/.worktrees/launchpad-pwragnt-main-moja6ucz","cliVersion":"0.125.0","source":"vscode","agentNickname":null,"agentRole":null,"gitInfo":{"sha":"329990027959f8a4d07fbce1ff7a804ba798fcb0","branch":null,"originUrl":"git@github.com:pwrdrvr/PwrAgnt.git"},"name":"Composer image paste reset E2E","turns":[{"id":"019dd682-6602-7042-9f9c-b1391b539fad","items":[{"type":"userMessage","id":"item-1","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Add a failing E2E test for creating a thread, posting a message, getting some responses, then pasting an image in the message composer, switching to another thread, coming back to the first thread, and seeing that the image is gone from the message composer.  This has been regularly failing for me.  So let's catch it, then let's fix it.","text_elements":[]}]},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-2","text":"I’ll trace the desktop E2E structure and composer/thread state handling first, then add the regression test in the same style before touching implementation. After I can reproduce the failure, I’ll patch the smallest state cleanup path and rerun the targeted test.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-3","text":"I found existing replay-backed E2E specs and a composer image normalization spec, so this can stay inside the desktop test harness instead of inventing new setup. I’m also going to use the project’s desktop fixture guidance because this test will need replay-like mocked app-server traffic.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-4","text":"The likely weak point is async paste handling: the attachment is added after normalization finishes, so a thread switch can race with that late state update. I’ll write the E2E to paste a real image and immediately switch threads after the preview appears or while the paste pipeline is active, then verify the first composer is empty when reselected.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-5","text":"I’m going to add the regression spec as a generated replay fixture inside the test file, matching the existing normalization and launchpad-sync tests. The key detail is a small test-only delay in canvas image encoding so the paste completion can land after a thread switch, which is the race this has been exposing.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-6","text":"The regression spec is in place now. I’ll install the workspace dependencies and then rerun the targeted spec.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-8","text":"The first E2E failure was fixture ordering, not the regression yet: the app never consumed my `skills/list` response before the live notifications. I’m removing that unnecessary response so the replay cursor reaches the turn notifications.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-9","text":"The test is now reaching the turn, but my synthetic assistant notification used the wrong protocol method. I’m switching it to the same `item/agentMessage/delta` shape used by the existing live-agent replay fixtures.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-10","text":"The first valid version passed because the assertion happened before the delayed paste finished. I’m tightening the test so it waits past the delayed image normalization, then asserts the attachment still did not leak back into the composer.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-11","text":"The regression is now failing as intended: after the delayed paste finishes, the image reappears in the first thread’s composer. I’m fixing the composer by tying each paste operation to the thread or launchpad scope that started it and discarding the async result if navigation changed before normalization completed.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-12","text":"The targeted E2E now passes after the fix. I’m running typecheck and a nearby composer-image E2E to catch any TypeScript or image upload regression from the scope guard.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-13","text":"The focused checks are clean, and the worktree only has the composer fix plus the new regression spec. Per your checkpoint preference, I’m making a signed commit for this isolated fix.","phase":"commentary","memoryCitation":null},{"type":"agentMessage","id":"item-14","text":"Implemented and committed the regression fix.

Commit: `213a4ac Fix pasted composer image thread switch race`

What changed:
- Added E2E coverage for: create thread, send initial message, receive streamed responses, paste image, switch away/back, verify composer image is gone.
- Fixed the race by scoping async pasted-image normalization to the active thread/launchpad and discarding late results after navigation.

Verified:
- Regression test failed before the fix with `Pasted images` count `1`.
- `pnpm --filter @pwragnt/desktop test:e2e -- composer-image-thread-switch.spec.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @pwragnt/desktop test:e2e -- composer-image-normalization.spec.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @pwragnt/desktop typecheck`

What changed:
- Per-thread unsent composer state now preserves draft text and pasted image attachments.
- If image normalization finishes after switching threads, the attachment is stored against the originating thread.
- PR title/body updated to match the actual fix.

Commit: `213a4ac Fix pasted composer image thread switch race`

What changed:
- Added E2E coverage for: create thread, send initial message, receive streamed responses, paste image, switch away/back, verify composer image is gone.
- Fixed the race by scoping async pasted-image normalization to the active thread/launchpad and discarding late results after navigation.

Verified:
- Regression test failed before the fix with `Pasted images` count `1`.
- `pnpm --filter @pwragnt/desktop test:e2e -- composer-image-thread-switch.spec.ts`

Review comment:

- [P2] Preserve async pasted images for launchpad scopes — /Users/huntharo/github/PwrAgnt/.worktrees/launchpad-pwragnt-main-moja6ucz/apps/desktop/src/renderer/src/features/composer/Composer.tsx:971-979
  When an image paste starts from a new-thread launchpad and the user switches away before `normalizeImageFile` resolves, this branch runs with a `launchpad:*` `pasteScope.key`; `saveThreadComposerDraft` ignores non-thread scopes, so the completed attachment is dropped instead of being persisted back to the launchpad draft.
